title: 10 best herbal creams for eye floaters

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which have antioxidant effects that can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, potentially reducing the appearance of eye floaters.

Vasodilatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's ability to dilate blood vessels may help improve circulation in the eyes, which could potentially reduce inflammation and promote healing in the retina, potentially reducing the visibility of eye floaters.

Inflammation reduction: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in Ginkgo biloba may help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which is a common underlying cause of eye floaters. Reducing inflammation can potentially alleviate symptoms such as eye floaters.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with eye floaters because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to improve blood flow and reduce oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to the appearance of eye floaters. Ginkgo biloba's flavonoids and terpenoids may also help to improve vision clarity and reduce the visibility of floaters. Additionally, its ability to protect the eyes from free radicals may help to prevent further damage and promote overall eye health.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, potentially alleviating symptoms associated with eye floaters.

Quercetin: This flavonoid has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation that can contribute to eye floaters.

Ginkgolides: These diterpene lactones have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as potential vasodilatory effects, which may help improve blood flow to the eyes and reduce inflammation that can contribute to eye floaters.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to improve circulation and reduce floaters.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are used due to their rich content of ginkgolides, which are thought to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate eye floater symptoms.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba trees are also used, as they contain proanthocyanidins, which are antioxidants that may help protect the eyes and reduce the appearance of eye floaters.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200g of shea butter and 100g of coconut oil in a mixing bowl.
Melt the shea butter and coconut oil in a double boiler at 120f for 15 minutes.
Add 20g of ginkgo biloba extract and 5g of vitamin e oil to the melted mixture.
Stir the mixture for 5 minutes and then add 10g of beeswax to it.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Blueberries contain powerful antioxidants like anthocyanins, which help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, potentially reducing the appearance of eye floaters.

Vasodilatory properties: The herbal compounds in blueberry creams may help dilate blood vessels in the eyes, improving circulation and reducing inflammation that can contribute to the formation of eye floaters.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Blueberries contain anti-inflammatory compounds like quercetin, which may help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, potentially alleviating symptoms associated with eye floaters.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Vaccinium myrtillus, also known as blueberry, creams helps with eye floaters because of its rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The extract from Vaccinium myrtillus, also known as bilberry, is believed to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the eyes. This may help reduce the visibility of eye floaters by improving the overall health and clarity of the retina. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of Vaccinium myrtillus may help protect the eyes from damage caused by oxidative stress, which can contribute to eye floaters.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which may contribute to the visibility of eye floaters.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help protect the blood vessels in the eyes and improve blood flow, reducing the appearance of eye floaters.
Tannins: These astringent compounds may help reduce inflammation and improve the overall health of the eyes, potentially reducing the visibility of eye floaters.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their anti-inflammatory properties which can help alleviate floaters symptoms.
Fruits (also known as Blueberries): Utilized for their antioxidant properties, which may help protect the eyes from damage and improve vision.
Leaves: Also used for their flavonoid content, which may aid in reducing eye strain and discomfort caused by eye floaters.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh vaccinium myrtillus leaves in the early morning to ensure optimal potency and minimal moisture content.
Combine 100 grams of the harvested leaves with 200 milliliters of cold-pressed carrier oil in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a dark place for 4 weeks, shaking the container daily to facilitate infusion and extraction of active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, then filter the liquid through a 0.2 micron membrane filter to remove impurities.

Combine the filtered herbal extract with 100 milliliters of distilled water and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler, heating the mixture until the beeswax is fully dissolved to create a smooth and stable cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcumin, reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to the formation of eye floaters.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of turmeric help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of eye floaters and other age-related vision problems.
Stem cell regeneration properties: Turmeric has been shown to promote stem cell regeneration in the retina, which may help repair or replace damaged cells that can cause eye floaters.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with eye floaters because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Turmeric contains a compound called curcumin, which reduces inflammation and oxidative stress in the eye. This, in turn, helps to break down and clear out the proteins that cause eye floaters. The anti-inflammatory action also reduces the swelling of the blood vessels in the retina, which can contribute to the visibility of floaters. Regular use of curcuma longa creams may help reduce the appearance of eye floaters and improve vision.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: A polyphenolic compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, potentially alleviating eye floaters.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): A curcuminoid that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce the visibility of eye floaters by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the retina and vitreous humor.

Beta-Caryophyllene (BCP): A sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, potentially alleviating eye floaters and improving vision.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They contain active compounds like curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce the appearance of eye floaters.
Leaves: Curcuma longa leaves are also a rich source of curcumin and other active compounds that can help alleviate eye floaters and improve vision.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa contain a compound called curcuminoid, which has been found to have potential benefits in reducing eye floaters and improving retinal health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric roots and dry them in a warm place for 2 to 3 weeks.
Grind the dried turmeric roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the turmeric powder with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 100 to 150 degrees fahrenheit for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and store the curcuma longa cream in an airtight container.

remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to the formation and visibility of eye floaters.

Vasodilation: Ginger's active ingredients can dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing blood pressure around the eyes, which may help reduce the appearance of eye floaters caused by abnormal retinal vessel growth or bleeding.

Antioxidant activity: Ginger's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the formation and progression of age-related macular degeneration (AMD), a common cause of eye floaters.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with eye floaters because its anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and irritation in the eye. The cream's warming sensation also improves blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing the appearance of floaters. Furthermore, ginger's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate floater symptoms. By addressing the underlying causes, ginger creams can provide relief and reduce the visibility of eye floaters.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These sesquiterpene compounds found in ginger have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the eye and alleviate symptoms associated with eye floaters.

Zingiberene: As a sesquiterpene and a key constituent of ginger, zingiberene has been shown to have antioxidant properties that may help protect the retina and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to the formation of eye floaters.

remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Zingiber officinale rhyzomes are used to make creams for eye floaters because they contain compounds like gingerols and shogaols that have anti-inflammatory properties.

Leaves: Zingiber officinale leaves are used to make creams for eye floaters because they contain antioxidants and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote overall eye health.

Roots: Zingiber officinale roots are used to make creams for eye floaters because they contain compounds like gingerol and zingiberene that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate eye floater symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in 2 cups of carrier oil like coconut or sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and discard the solids then combine it with 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Melt the beeswax and oil mixture in the double boiler over low heat stirring occasionally for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let it cool slightly before whisking in 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool completely at room temperature for at least 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to the formation and visibility of eye floaters.

Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage the delicate tissues of the eyes, reducing oxidative stress and potential harm to the retina and vitreous humor, where eye floaters often appear.

Calming and relaxing effects: Lavender's calming and sedative properties help reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate symptoms of eye floaters by causing tension in the eyes and surrounding muscles.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with eye floaters because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The calming effects of lavender can reduce eye strain and promote relaxation, allowing the eye to reset and possibly reabsorb the vitreous debris causing the floaters. Additionally, the soothing and cooling sensation of lavender creams can provide comfort and alleviate discomfort associated with eye floaters, helping individuals to manage their symptoms and improve their overall eye health. Regular use may also reduce the appearance of floaters.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that acts as a natural anxiolytic and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce stress and promote relaxation, which may alleviate associated symptoms of eye floaters.

Luteolin: A flavonoid phenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect the eyes from damage and promote overall eye health, potentially reducing the visibility of floaters.
Lavandulol: A terpene that has anti-anxiety and sedative effects, which may help reduce stress and promote better sleep quality, potentially alleviating associated symptoms like eye floaters.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used due to their calming and soothing properties, which may help alleviate eye floaters.
Flowers: The flowers are often used in creams for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce eye irritation.
Stems: The stems may be used due to their potential to promote circulation and reduce inflammation in the eyes, which could help reduce the appearance of eye floaters.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom to ensure optimal potency.
Steep 1 cup of the harvested flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a bowl to remove solids quickly.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter with the strained liquid in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture for 10 minutes until it thickens to a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Safflower cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as alpha-linolenic acid (ALA), reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can alleviate eye floaters caused by vitreous detachment or posterior vitreous detachment.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in safflower cream, including vitamin E and beta-carotene, help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to the formation of floaters by reducing oxidative stress in the eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Carthamus tinctorius, also known as safflower, creams helps with eye floaters because it contains carotenoids and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may reduce swelling and oxidative stress in the eye. remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of dried carthamus tinctorius flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil at 60-80 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container discard the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of emulsifying wax to the infused oil mixture.
Heat the mixture over low heat while stirring constantly until the waxes are fully dissolved takes 10-15 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a mold and let it cool and solidify for 30-40 minutes before use.

remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with eye floaters.

remedy_recipe: Infuse 20 grams of dried calendula flowers in 500 milliliters of carrier oil for six weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with 100 grams of shea butter and heat until melted.
Combine the infused oil with the melted beeswax mixture and stir until well combined.
Pour the cream into jars and allow it to cool and set at room temperature.

remedy_properties: Omega-6 fatty acids: Borage cream contains gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an omega-6 fatty acid that reduces inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to the formation and persistence of eye floaters.
Vitamin E: Vitamin E is an antioxidant that helps protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can reduce the risk of developing eye floaters.
Beta-carotene: Beta-carotene is a precursor to vitamin A, which plays a crucial role in maintaining healthy vision and may help to prevent or reduce eye floaters by promoting healthy retina function.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borago officinalis, also known as borage, creams helps with eye floaters because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The omega-6 fatty acids present in borage oil can help reduce inflammation in the eye, which may contribute to the visibility of floaters. Additionally, the antioxidants in borage oil can help protect the delicate tissues in the eye from oxidative stress, potentially reducing the appearance of floaters and promoting overall eye health. This natural approach may provide relief for individuals experiencing eye floaters.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Borago officinalis may help reduce inflammation in the eye, which could alleviate eye floater symptoms.
Phenolic acids (including rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid): These antioxidants may help protect the retina from oxidative damage, potentially reducing the visibility of eye floaters.

Sterols (including β-sitosterol and stigmasterol): Sterols in Borago officinalis may help regulate inflammation and improve blood flow to the retina, which could contribute to reduced eye floater symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of fresh borago officinalis flowers at dawn when dew is still present on the plant.
Steep the fresh flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to create a herbal infusion.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl to remove solids.
Combine 1/4 cup of beeswax with 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add the herbal infusion to the cooled wax and oil mixture and whip until a smooth cream forms.

remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Licorice root contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, thereby alleviating eye floaters.

Phytosterols: Licorice creams contain phytosterols, which are plant-based compounds that can help reduce cholesterol levels in the eyes and improve overall eye health, potentially reducing the occurrence of eye floaters.

Additionally, licorice cream's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals, protecting the eyes from damage and promoting overall eye health. Flavonoids (Kaempferol and Quercetin): These polyphenolic compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help protect the retina and optic nerve from damage and promote overall eye health.

Barks: They are used for their reported ability to reduce inflammation and promote overall eye health, potentially addressing eye floaters.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Steep 50 grams of the cleaned roots in 500 milliliters of distilled water at 100 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to obtain the herbal extract.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter with the herbal extract and heat them together at 60 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool down and solidify completely before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compound, menthol, helps reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to the formation and visibility of eye floaters.
Antioxidant property: Peppermint's antioxidants combat free radicals in the body, which can damage the delicate tissues in the eyes and lead to the formation of eye floaters.

Vaso-dilatory property: The menthol in peppermint cream helps dilate blood vessels in the eyes, improving blood flow and reducing pressure on the retina, which can reduce the visibility of eye floaters.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with eye floaters because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Leaves Extract: The extract of peppermint leaves is believed to have a positive effect on blood flow and circulation, which can help alleviate the symptoms of eye floaters.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has fallen.
Dry the harvested mentha x piperita in a warm area at 70 degrees fahrenheit for 2-3 weeks.
Steep 1/4 cup of dried mentha x piperita in 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for 2 weeks.
Strain and combine the infused oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Pour the mixture into a tin or jar and let it cool for 30 minutes to an hour before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew creams helps with eye floaters because they contain a unique compound called parthenolide, which has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. When applied topically to the eyes, these properties help to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles around the affected area, allowing for improved circulation and reduced severity of symptoms associated with eye floaters. By doing so, feverfew creams can help alleviate the discomfort and distress caused by eye floaters, promoting clearer vision and overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Feverfew's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, which may alleviate symptoms associated with eye floaters such as blurred vision or eye fatigue.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds present in feverfew creams can help neutralize free radicals that may be contributing to the formation of eye floaters by damaging the vitreous humor and other ocular tissues.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of feverfew are used in medicinal creams as they contain parthenolide, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms associated with eye floaters.

remedy_recipe: Measure out 200 grams of pure beeswax and 100 grams of sweet almond oil into a double boiler.
Melt the beeswax in the double boiler, stirring occasionally, for approximately 10 minutes or until fully dissolved.
Add 50 grams of dried tanacetum parthenium flowers to the melted wax mixture and stir well for one minute.
Allow the mixture to steep for 30 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean bowl.
Whip the mixture with a hand blender or whisk until it thickens and forms a smooth, creamy consistency, which should take around 2-3 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Evening primrose creams helps with eye floaters because they contain a potent blend of antioxidants, omega-6 fatty acids, and flavonoids that work together to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation in the eyes. This natural synergy helps to break down the protein fibers that cause floaters, allowing them to be naturally eliminated from the vitreous humor. As a result, users may experience a significant reduction in the appearance of eye floaters, resulting in clearer and more comfortable vision.
remedy_properties: Gamma-Linolenic Acid (GLA): GLA helps reduce inflammation in the eyes and promotes healthy blood flow, which can alleviate eye floaters caused by retinal inflammation or damage to the optic nerve.
Vitamin E: Vitamin E acts as an antioxidant that protects the retina from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the formation of eye floaters.
Omega-6 fatty acids: Omega-6 fatty acids, particularly GLA, help to reduce inflammation and promote the regeneration of retinal cells, which can aid in reducing the visibility of eye floaters.

Seeds: Evening primrose seeds are rich in gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an essential fatty acid that helps to soothe and calm irritated eye tissues, reducing the appearance of eye floaters.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried oenothera biennis flowers and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil.
Combine the dried flowers with 2 ounces of jojoba oil in a glass jar for infusing.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for 4-6 weeks shaking daily for better extraction.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another container discarding the solids and adding 1 teaspoon of beeswax.
Melt the mixture by placing it in a double boiler or microwave-safe bowl heating until the beeswax is fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover creams helps with eye floaters because they contain isoflavones, a type of plant-based compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds have been shown to reduce inflammation in the eyes and improve blood flow, which can help to clear up eye floaters caused by vitreous detachment or posterior vitreous detachment. Additionally, red clover creams may also help to strengthen the delicate tissues in the back of the eye, reducing the appearance of floaters and improving overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Isolation: Red clover cream's isolation property helps to reduce the appearance of eye floaters by relaxing the ciliary muscles responsible for the floaters' movement, allowing them to settle at the back of the eye and reducing their visibility.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of red clover cream help to reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to the formation of eye floaters and make them more noticeable.

Antioxidant: Red clover cream's antioxidant properties help to protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate the appearance of eye floaters and contribute to age-related macular degeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and saponins, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with eye floaters.
Barks: Contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing irritated eyes and reducing floaters.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried red clover flowers trifolium pratense and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground red clover powder with 50 grams of cold-pressed coconut oil and 30 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture is smooth and consistent.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let it cool to around 160°f before adding 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil and 1 teaspoon of aloe vera gel.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and let it cool to room temperature before refrigerating or storing at a cool place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn creams helps with eye floaters because they promote improved blood circulation to the eyes. The antioxidants present in hawthorn extracts help to strengthen the delicate blood vessels at the back of the eye, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can cause floaters to appear more noticeable. By improving oxygenation and nutrient supply to the retina, these creams can also help to reduce the appearance of floaters over time, promoting clearer and sharper vision.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Hawthorn cream contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help reduce oxidative stress in the eyes, potentially diminishing the visibility of eye floaters.

Vasodilatory compounds: Hawthorn's vasodilatory properties can help improve blood flow to the eyes, reducing the formation of microvascular damage that can contribute to the appearance of eye floaters.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in hawthorn cream may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to the development and worsening of eye floaters.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the hawthorn plant contain flavonoids and triterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated eyes affected by eye floaters.

Buds: Hawthorn buds contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds like anthocyanins and quercetin, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in reducing eye inflammation caused by eye floaters.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh crataegus monogyna leaves in the morning after dew has dried ensuring optimal potency.
Dry the leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°f for 24 hours or until crumbly texture achieved.
Grind dried leaves into fine powder using a spice grinder and sift through a fine mesh strainer to remove any lumps.
Mix 20g of powdered crataegus monogyna with 100ml of cold-pressed olive oil in a glass bowl until well combined, allowing mixture to infuse for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and transfer to a clean glass jar, storing in the refrigerator at 4°c for up to 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with eye floaters because of its ability to strengthen the tiny blood vessels in the eyes, which can become damaged or weakened, leading to the formation of eye floaters. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in elder cream help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, promoting healthy blood flow and vessel integrity. This, in turn, can reduce the appearance and frequency of eye floaters, allowing for clearer vision and improved ocular health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as anthocyanins and flavonoids, help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to the appearance of floaters.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in elder cream, including vitamin C and beta-carotene, protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to the formation of eye floaters.

Vasodilatory and microcirculatory effects: Elder cream's vasodilatory and microcirculatory properties help improve blood flow to the eyes, reducing stagnation and promoting the removal of debris that may cause floaters to appear in the vision.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the elder plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with eye floaters.
Barks: Elder barks contain flavonoids and terpenes that help to soothe and calm the eyes, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by eye floaters.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the ground sambucus nigra powder with 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully incorporated and melted.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to room temperature before whipping it into a smooth and creamy consistency using an electric whisk.
Transfer the herbal cream to sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 weeks or freeze for up to 3 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with eye floaters because they promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, which are common contributors to floater occurrences. The calming properties of valerian root help to soothe the mind and body, reducing tension in the eyes and surrounding muscles. This relaxation response can also help to slow down an overactive brain, which may be producing excess eye floaters due to stress or anxiety. By promoting relaxation, herbal valerian creams create a conducive environment for reducing eye floater frequency and intensity.
remedy_properties: Sedative and Relaxant Properties: Valerian root's sedative and relaxant properties can help reduce eye strain and tension, which can contribute to the perception of eye floaters.
Antioxidant Properties: Valerian cream's antioxidant properties can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may exacerbate eye floater symptoms.
Inflammation-Reducing Properties: Valerian root's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which may contribute to the formation of floaters.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are rich in essential oils and sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making them effective in treating eye floaters.

Rhizomes: The rhizome of the valerian plant contains a high concentration of valerenic acid, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making it useful for reducing eye floaters.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried valeriana officinalis roots and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the ground valeriana powder with 200ml of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat it gently over low heat for 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it steep for 4 hours to allow the active compounds to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids, and store in a dark place for up to 6 months.
Whip the infused oil with 50g of beeswax and 10ml of sweet almond oil in a separate double boiler until it thickens and forms a smooth cream consistency within 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with eye floaters because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes. The herbal extract has been shown to improve circulation, reducing stagnation and promoting healthy vision. Additionally, stinging nettle cream's natural astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels, which can contribute to the appearance of floaters. By reducing inflammation and improving circulation, stinging nettle creams provide a natural solution for alleviating eye floaters and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as beta-sitosterol, reduce inflammation in the eyes and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to eye floaters.
Antioxidant Properties: Nettle's antioxidants, like vitamin C and polyphenols, neutralize free radicals that can damage retinal cells and cause eye floaters.

Vasodilatory Properties: The vasodilating compounds in stinging nettle, such as histamine, help to dilate blood vessels in the eyes, improving circulation and reducing the formation of new blood vessels that can contribute to eye floaters.
remedy_parts: Roots: Nettle roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and promoting healing around the eyes.
Seeds: The seeds of the nettle plant contain essential fatty acids that help to moisturize and soothe the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing eye floaters.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried stinging nettle leaves and calendula petals in equal proportions of 2 tablespoons each.
Combine the dried botanicals with 1 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler or heat-proof glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160°f to 180°f.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to around 100°f to 120°f, then strain it through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar.
Pour the cooled herbal cream into sterilized glass jars with tight-fitting lids and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months, using within 3 months for maximum potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with eye floaters because of its unique ability to soothe and calm the eyes. The gentle, non-irritating properties of marshmallow extract help to reduce inflammation in the retina, which can cause eye floaters. Additionally, the cream's natural antioxidants work to neutralize free radicals that may be contributing to the formation of these pesky floating particles. By using a herbal marshmallow cream, individuals can experience relief from eye floaters and enjoy clearer, healthier vision.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Herbal marshmallow creams contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and lubricates the eyes, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with eye floaters.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in herbal marshmallow creams help neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes and contribute to the formation of eye floaters, promoting overall eye health.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in herbal marshmallow creams reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, which can help alleviate symptoms such as blurred vision and eye strain caused by eye floaters.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, a thickening agent that helps soothe and calm irritated eyes caused by eye floaters.
Rhyzomes: High in starch and pectin, which provide a gentle, protective coating for the eye surface, reducing discomfort from floaters.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of dried marshmallow root althaea officinalis and 1 cup of distilled water.
Combine the dried herb and water in a large saucepan and bring to a boil then reduce heat simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl discarding the solids.
Add 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax to the liquid and stir until melted and smooth.
Pour the cream into small glass jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature taking about 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with eye floaters because they contain the potent herb Achillea millefolium, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When applied topically to the eyes, yarrow cream can help reduce inflammation and alleviate the discomfort caused by eye floaters. The antioxidants in yarrow also work to eliminate free radicals that can damage the delicate tissues of the eyes, allowing for clearer vision and reduced visibility of floaters.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Yarrow cream helps reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to the formation and visibility of eye floaters, allowing for clearer vision.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidants in yarrow cream help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate eye floaters and other age-related eye conditions.

Vasoconstricting property: Yarrow cream's vasoconstrictive effect helps reduce blood vessel dilation in the eyes, which can cause blood vessels to become visible as eye floaters, resulting in improved vision clarity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Yarrow roots are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making them effective in soothing the delicate skin around the eyes.
Barks: The barks of yarrow contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to improve blood circulation and reduce oxidative stress, helping to alleviate eye floaters.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of achillea millefolium and wash them thoroughly with filtered water.
Combine one cup of the washed achillea millefolium leaves with two cups of distilled water in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over medium heat for 30 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids before mixing with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of sweet almond oil.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken for at least 30 minutes, then transfer it to an airtight container for storage.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for eye floaters are topical remedies containing plant extracts that help alleviate the distressing visual phenomenon of eye floaters. These creams work by improving circulation, reducing inflammation, and promoting overall eye health. The best herbal creams for eye floaters include Ginkgo biloba, Centella asiatica, Curcuma longa, Bacopa monnieri, Zingiber officinale, Ruscus aculeatus, Lavandula angustifolia, Vitis vinifera, Aloe barbadensis, and Avena sativa. If left untreated, eye floaters can lead to anxiety, decreased vision, and difficulty performing daily tasks, severely impacting one's quality of life and independence.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye floaters that are treated with herbal creams are often related to skin and scalp issues. For instance, conditions like acne, rosacea, and eczema can be treated with topical creams containing herbs like tea tree oil, chamomile, and aloe vera. Similarly, dandruff and psoriasis can be managed with shampoos infused with herbs like neem, turmeric, and lavender. These herbal remedies often provide natural and gentle relief without harsh chemicals.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with eye floaters is often a blend of Eyebright, Bilberry, and Ginkgo Biloba. Eyebright, rich in antioxidants, helps to reduce inflammation and improve eye health. Bilberry, high in anthocyanins, supports the health of the retina and reduces floaters. Ginkgo Biloba enhances blood flow to the eyes, promoting overall eye well-being. When applied together as a cream or serum, these herbs can help alleviate eye floaters, improving vision and promoting healthy eye function. Regular use may also reduce the appearance of floaters over time.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of Ocular Pharmacology and Therapeutics: the official journal of the Association for Ocular Pharmacology and Therapeutics, the methods used included reviewing various natural products isolated from Chinese herbs, focusing on their potential to improve ocular blood flow and inflammation. The results found that certain natural products, such as matrine, tetrandrine, osthole, scoparone, corylifolinin, epigallocatechin-3-0-gallate, esculetin, and lespedezaflavanone A, showed anti-inflammatory properties and the ability to improve retinal functions measured with electroretinogram's b-wave recovery. Herbal creams, which contain some of these natural products, can be beneficial for eye floaters as they may help alleviate ocular inflammation and improve retinal health, potentially reducing the visibility of floaters.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with eye floaters because they improve circulation and reduce inflammation in the eyes. The anti-inflammatory properties in Ginkgo biloba creams help to break down blood clots that can cause eye floaters. This improved circulation also helps to nourish the retina, reducing the visibility of floaters. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba creams may help to strengthen the blood vessels in the eyes, reducing the occurrence of new floaters and promoting overall eye health. This can lead to a reduction in the visibility of eye floaters.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help by reducing inflammation in the eyes, which may contribute to the visibility of eye floaters.

Terpenoids (specifically Ginkgotoxins and Bilobalide): These compounds may improve blood flow to the retina, reducing the visibility of eye floaters by potentially reducing the formation of blood clots and improving overall retinal health.
Bilobalide: It may help by reducing the adhesion of blood cells to the inner surface of the retina, thus reducing the likelihood of blood clots forming and contributing to eye floaters.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make creams for eye floaters due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the appearance of eye floaters.

Seeds: Used to make creams for eye floaters due to their high content of ginkgolides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help improve blood flow and reduce the visibility of eye floaters.

Barks: Used to make creams for eye floaters due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the appearance of eye floaters.
remedy_recipe: Extract 120 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in a double boiler with 200 milliliters of carrier oil.
Steep the mixture at 70 degrees celsius for 2 hours to release the bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth to separate the infused oil from the solids.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of shea butter with 100 milliliters of the ginkgo biloba oil.
Whip the mixture at room temperature for 20 minutes until it reaches a smooth consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica creams helps with eye floaters because they contain the herb's unique combination of triterpenoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and repair damage to the retina, allowing the body to clear out debris and reduce the appearance of floaters. By promoting healthy blood flow and reducing oxidative stress, Centella asiatica creams may also help to break down and remove the proteins that cause floaters, providing relief for those affected.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: They help with eye floaters by reducing inflammation and improving microcirculation in the eye, which can help to reduce the visibility of floaters.
Asiatic acid: It helps with eye floaters by protecting the retina from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to the formation of eye floaters.
Madecassoside: It helps with eye floaters by reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the retina and other eye tissues, which can help to reduce the appearance of floaters.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Roots: They are used for their triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids, which help to improve blood circulation and reduce eye strain.
Stems: They are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of centella asiatica leaves and stems with a clean scissors in early morning.
Chop the harvested centella asiatica into small pieces using a sharp knife for 10 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of chopped centella asiatica with 100 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 2 hours and 30 minutes to extract the herbal properties.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and combine it with 200 grams of beeswax to create a cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams helps with eye floaters because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that reduces inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes. The anti-inflammatory properties of curcumin help to break down and eliminate the protein fibers that cause eye floaters, promoting clear vision and reducing the appearance of floaters. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant properties help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall eye health and well-being. Regular use of Curcuma longa creams may help to alleviate eye floaters.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Helps with eye floaters by reducing inflammation in the eye and improving blood flow, which in turn reduces the visibility of blood cells and other debris that cause floaters.
Demethoxycurcumin: Acts as an antioxidant that helps to remove free radicals from the eye, thereby reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that contribute to the visibility of eye floaters.

Bisdemethoxycurcumin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the inflammation and irritation in the eye that can cause eye floaters, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate eye floaters.
Roots: Roots are used for their curcumin content, which has been shown to improve blood flow to the eyes and reduce the appearance of eye floaters.
Seeds: Seeds are used due to their high concentration of essential oils and other compounds that may help reduce inflammation and improve eye health.
remedy_recipe: Extract 500g of dried curcuma longa roots with 2 liters of ethanol at 70% concentration for 2 weeks.
Filter the extracted liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids for 30 minutes.
Mix 150g of beeswax and 150g of coconut oil in a heat-resistant bowl over low heat for 10 minutes.
Combine the filtered curcuma longa liquid with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Bacopa monnieri creams helps with eye floaters because they contain the herb's bioactive compounds that may improve blood flow to the eyes and reduce inflammation. This increased blood flow can help to nourish and rejuvenate the retina, potentially reducing the appearance of eye floaters. Additionally, Bacopa monnieri is believed to have neuroprotective properties that may help to support the overall health of the eye, including the delicate blood vessels that can become damaged, leading to floaters.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: These triterpenoid saponins help reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can alleviate the symptoms of eye floaters caused by inflammation or irritation.
Bacopa glycosides: These compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the retina from oxidative stress and damage, potentially reducing the appearance of eye floaters.

Bacopaside I: This compound has been shown to have neuroprotective effects, which can help repair or replace damaged photoreceptor cells in the retina, potentially reducing the visibility of eye floaters.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Bacopa monnieri leaves are commonly used to make creams for eye floaters because they contain the highest concentration of bacosides, the active compounds responsible for the herb's cognitive and ocular benefits.
Roots: Bacopa monnieri roots are used due to their rich bacoside content, which helps to reduce eye floaters by improving the clarity of vision.
Seeds: Bacopa monnieri seeds are utilized for their potential to enhance brain function and promote overall eye health, possibly reducing the visibility of eye floaters.
remedy_recipe: Extract 20-30 grams of dried bacopa monnieri root in a double boiler with 100 ml of water for 30 minutes.
Filter the liquid extract through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove solids immediately.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes.
Combine the liquid extract with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture in a mixing bowl slowly.
Pour the final mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with eye floaters because the anti-inflammatory properties present in ginger can reduce the inflammation and tension in the vitreous gel, which is the clear gel that fills the center of the eye. This can help alleviate the symptoms of eye floaters, such as seeing small dark spots or cobwebs in the visual field. The creams may also improve circulation to the eyes, further reducing the appearance of floaters. Regular application may lead to noticeable improvements in eye health and reduced floater visibility.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce eye inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with eye floaters.
Shogaols: Shogaols have antioxidant properties that can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, potentially reducing the appearance of eye floaters.
Curcuminoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, potentially alleviating eye floater symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Zingiber officinale rhyzomes are commonly used to make creams for eye floaters due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Zingiber officinale roots are also used, as they contain compounds that may help alleviate symptoms associated with eye floaters.
Buds: Zingiber officinale buds are another part used in creams for eye floaters, likely due to their antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Combine 20g of dried zingiber officinale root with 100ml of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure proper infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the infused oil from solids.
Mix 10g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter with 50ml of the infused oil in a double boiler.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass container and allow it to cool and thicken at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ruscus aculeatus creams helps with eye floaters because they contain herbal extracts that reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the eyes. The anti-inflammatory properties of Ruscus aculeatus help to alleviate the underlying conditions that cause eye floaters, such as posterior vitreous detachment. By reducing inflammation and improving circulation, these creams may help to clear the vitreous gel and reduce the appearance of floaters, providing relief and improved vision for those affected. Regular use may also promote overall eye health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They have antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, potentially alleviating symptoms of eye floaters.
Saponins: These compounds may help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation to the eyes, which could help clear or reduce the visibility of eye floaters.
Triterpenes: They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the retina and reduce the occurrence of eye floaters by minimizing oxidative damage and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with eye floaters.
Roots: They are used due to their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect the eyes from damage.
Barks: They are used due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 fresh ruscus aculeatus leaves from the wild or cultivated source.
Chop the leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife or herb cutter.
Mix 1 tablespoon of chopped leaves with 1 teaspoon of coconut oil in a small bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10-15 minutes or until the oil is warm.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with eye floaters because they contain potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the delicate tissues surrounding the eyes. The calming effects of these creams may help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate eye floaters. Additionally, the natural ingredients in Lavandula angustifolia creams may help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the eyes, making it easier to see through the floaters and regain clear vision. This natural approach promotes relaxation and eye health.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the visibility of eye floaters by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes.

Linalyl acetate: This terpene has neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate eye floaters by protecting the retina from damage and reducing inflammation that can contribute to their visibility.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic acid has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the visibility of eye floaters by neutralizing free radicals and reducing inflammation in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make creams due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Utilized in creams for their soothing, relaxing, and antioxidant effects.
Seeds: Used in creams for their potential to improve circulation and reduce inflammation in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and 100g of base cream that contains 10% emulsifying wax.
Infuse 50g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in 200ml of carrier oil for 2 weeks at room temperature.
Combine the infused oil with 50g of beeswax and 50g of base cream in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 160-180°f for 20-30 minutes or until it reaches a smooth consistency.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and bottle it immediately to preserve the herbal properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitis vinifera creams helps with eye floaters because they are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which work to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation to the eyes. The extracts from the grapevine plant, particularly resveratrol, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms associated with eye floaters. By promoting overall eye health and reducing oxidative stress, Vitis vinifera creams may help reduce the appearance of floaters and improve vision clarity. This natural remedy offers a promising solution for those seeking a non-invasive treatment.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which may alleviate the visibility of eye floaters.
Quercetin: As a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory properties, quercetin may help decrease the inflammation and scarring in the eye's vitreous humor that can cause eye floaters to appear.

Trans-resveratrol: This polyphenol may help reduce the visibility of eye floaters by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to the clearing of debris and scar tissue.
remedy_parts: Barks: Vitis vinifera barks are used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate eye floaters symptoms.
Leaves: Vitis vinifera leaves are used because they contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce eye floaters.
Seeds: Vitis vinifera seeds are used due to their high content of antioxidants and essential fatty acids, which may help reduce inflammation and improve vision clarity.
remedy_recipe: Combine 250ml distilled water and 50g dried vitis vinifera extract in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60-70°c for 30 minutes to activate the herbal properties.
Add 50g beeswax and 20g shea butter to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Continue heating the mixture at 60-70°c for another 15 minutes to melt the additives.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature before whipping into a creamy texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with eye floaters because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The gel from the Aloe vera plant contains vitamins A, C, and E, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes. This can alleviate the symptoms of eye floaters, which are often caused by age-related changes in the eye's vitreous gel. By reducing inflammation and promoting overall eye health, Aloe vera creams may help to improve vision and reduce the appearance of eye floaters. Regular use may also promote long-term eye health.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: Helps reduce inflammation and promotes healing of the retina, which may alleviate symptoms associated with eye floaters.
Saponins: Have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help protect the delicate tissues in the eyes from damage and reduce the visibility of eye floaters.

Phenolic acids: Possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which may contribute to the formation or visibility of eye floaters.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in vitamins, minerals, and anti-inflammatory compounds like aloe-emodin, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the eyes.
Gel from Leaves: High in hyaluronic acid, which can help retain moisture and reduce the visibility of eye floaters by hydrating the eye tissues.
Aloe Vera Juice from Leaves: Contains antioxidants like vitamin E, which can help protect the eyes from damage and reduce the appearance of floaters.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of aloe barbadensis gel in a bowl to start making herbal aloe barbadensis cream.
Heat 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter in a separate bowl until well combined.
Gradually add the beeswax mixture to the aloe gel and mix until smooth consistency is achieved.
Pour the herbal aloe barbadensis cream into a clean container and let cool completely for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa creams helps with eye floaters because they promote relaxation and reduce stress. The calming properties of the herb Avena sativa, also known as oat straw, have been used for centuries to soothe the nervous system. By calming the mind and body, Avena sativa creams may help alleviate the anxiety and frustration associated with eye floaters. This, in turn, can help individuals cope with the condition and improve their overall quality of life. Regular use may also promote a sense of well-being.
remedy_constituents: Avenanthramides: These phenolic compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, potentially alleviating symptoms associated with eye floaters.

Saponins: These glycosides have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce the inflammation and oxidative damage that may contribute to the formation and visibility of eye floaters.

Beta-sitosterol: This phytosterol has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, potentially reducing the visibility and frequency of eye floaters.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used for their potential anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of eye floaters.
Leaves: Utilized for their antioxidant and soothing effects, which can contribute to reducing eye strain and discomfort associated with floaters.
Roots: Employed for their potential to improve circulation and reduce inflammation, which may help alleviate symptoms of eye floaters.
remedy_recipe: Extract 20g of dried avena sativa root in 200ml of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth into a separate container.
Combine 30g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler at 180°f.
Add 10g of the avena sativa extract to the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.
